text.title: Linear Issue Tracking (Project Manager) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Linear Issue Tracking (Project Manager). Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: PM triages 'No Status' inbox daily (≤10 min). Issues either go to a cycle, the backlog, or are cancelled.
rule.rationale: Daily triage prevents 'No Status' from becoming the largest column.

rule.statement: Three cycle ceremonies: planning (kickoff), mid-cycle check-in, retro. Skipping any = drift.
rule.rationale: Three is the minimum cadence that keeps cycle commitments honest.

rule.statement: Monthly label review: delete labels with 0 issues in last 30 days; merge near-duplicates.
rule.rationale: Label sprawl is gradual; monthly cull keeps the taxonomy ≤15.

rule.statement: Weekly status email/post uses fixed template: shipped / in-progress / blockers / next-week / risks.
rule.rationale: Templated status is scannable + comparable across weeks.

rule.statement: Quarterly velocity + cycle-completion review; if completion <0.65 across 4 cycles, recompute capacity.
rule.rationale: Persistent under-completion signals capacity miscalc, not 'just a bad quarter'.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-linear-issue-tracking.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
